mechanism_narrative: A sustained near-zero federal funds rate suppresses the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, inflating equity multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% are not positive in >55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month window signals accelerating labor market healing, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE declines by >0.2pp over 3 months, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered months, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm a broad demand recovery across consumption and manufacturing sectors, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns more reliably than either proxy in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO month-over-month changes produces a Rank IC below 0.04 versus SPX 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime, month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reduces recession probability and boosts aggregate income expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns; this effect disappears when labor markets are tight.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6% and PAYEMS increases month-over-month, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered quarters over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP translates into higher aggregate corporate revenue forecasts and lower recession probability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and SPX 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing above the 1000 thousand-unit threshold signals a durable recovery in residential investment and construction employment, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In months where HOUST exceeds 1000, SPX 5-day forward returns are not positive in >55% of instances over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in headline CPI erodes real earnings expectations and raises discount rate risk, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following month-over-month CPIAUCSL acceleration are not negative in >50% of months, or the Rank IC is above -0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly increases in industrial production signal rising capacity utilization and manufacturing operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered instances over a rolling 2-year window.
